# Git Submodule Documentation Pattern (LLM Guide)

## Concept
Decouple documentation and source code into separate repositories:
1. **Library Repository** (`my-library`): Contains the code source.
2. **Docs Repository** (`my-library-docs`): Contains markdown manuals, guides, and api-specs.

## Workflow

### Phase A: Setup in Library Repository (Write access)
1. Create a separate documentation repository (e.g., `my-library-docs`).
2. Add it as a writable submodule under `docs/releases`:
   `git submodule add git@github.com:my-organization/my-library-docs.git docs/releases`
3. Document features and tag documentation releases synchronously with library releases.

### Phase B: Consumption in Consumer Repository (Read access)
1. Add the docs repository as a read-only submodule under `docs/sdk-docs/my-library`:
   `git submodule add git@github.com:my-organization/my-library-docs.git docs/sdk-docs/my-library`
2. Fetch tags and pin to the exact version used in the project:
   ```bash
   cd docs/sdk-docs/my-library
   git fetch --tags
   git checkout tags/v1.0.0
   ```
3. Commit the pinned submodule to the consumer repository.

## Benefits for AI Agents
Autonomously operating agents in the consumer workspace can scan `docs/sdk-docs/` to immediately find version-accurate documentation without crawling code or external search engines.
